decisions.decision: Haiku-only classification — Sonnet escalation removed entirely
decisions.rationale: Logs showed ~91% escalation rate due to 0.8 confidence threshold. Haiku calibration returns 0.6-0.79 even on clear-cut emails.

decisions.decision: Conversation tracking system rules disabled (TO_REPLY, AWAITING_REPLY, FYI, ACTIONED)
decisions.rationale: These rules triggered aiDetermineThreadStatus on every person-email, which used modelType='default'=Sonnet. Observed $0.016/email vs $0.004/email target. Disabling saves ~$0.013/person-email. Feature can be re-enabled when/if a cost budget is allocated for it.

decisions.decision: Bulk Unsubscribe (existing feature) preferred over new sender-triage UI for cost reduction
decisions.rationale: Bulk Unsubscribe already shows newsletter senders with counts and creates Gmail filters (truly $0 future cost). Should be explored before building new feature. Gap: depends on senders being in the Newsletter table.

decisions.decision: Prompt caching deferred — low-value at current email volume
decisions.rationale: At 85 emails/day (~1 per 10 min), most calls arrive after the 5-min Anthropic cache TTL expires. Savings would be 20-30% during bursts only. Not worth the code change now.

next_action: Send 8 test emails per 03-05-PLAN.md Task 1, run psql queries A-G, paste results in chat and reply 'verified'
context_notes: Cost crisis this session: $5 credit went to $4.07 in <24h. Root cause found and fixed: 4 conversation tracking rules (TO_REPLY/AWAITING_REPLY/FYI/ACTIONED) were triggering a second Sonnet call per person-email via aiDetermineThreadStatus. Disabled in DB. Cost should now be ~$0.004/email (~$10/month). Phase 3 verification (Wave 5, plan 03-05) still pending — all 3 tasks are manual checkpoints. Credits now sufficient to proceed.
